name: SOUNDTHINKING, INC.
business: SoundThinking is a public safety technology company that provides a platform of data-driven solutions including gunshot detection, crime data analysis, case management, resource deployment, license plate recognition, and weapons detection, sold primarily to law enforcement and government agencies on a subscription basis.
sellsTo: police departments of major cities in the United States
federal agencies, foreign governments and higher education institutions
universities, corporate campuses, hospitals, casinos and key infrastructure and transportation centers

exposures.sentence: Our gunshot detection solutions consist of highly-specialized, cloud-based software integrated with proprietary, internet-enabled sensors designed to detect outdoor gunfire.
SafePointe, an AI-based weapons detection system designed to provide discreet, high-throughput screening that complements physical security measures without compromising visitor experience.

Research and development expense increased by $1.9 million, primarily due to an increase of $1.0 million in consulting expense associated with SafePointe and a $0.9 million increase in IT expense related to our investments in enhancing our AI capabilities.
Our primary IRC and a data center that hosts some of our customer services are located in the San Francisco Bay Area, a region known for seismic activity.
In the fourth quarter of 2024, an audit of our processes under a SOC2 Type 2 audit was completed.
In July 2024, we announced a strategic partnership to create and launch a new end-to-end vehicle and ALPR public safety solution, PlateRanger, Powered by Rekor.

catalysts.event: Launch of perimeter-based sniper gunshot detection solution targeting utility substations
catalysts.sentence: In the first quarter of 2025, we rolled out a perimeter-based sniper gunshot detection solution targeting utility substations, with initial pilots aimed at utility customers, conducted through SoundThinking Labs.

catalysts.event: California AB 2975 mandate requiring weapons detection systems in hospitals by 2027
catalysts.sentence: We have an opportunity to grow in the healthcare vertical with California’s AB 2975 mandate, which requires weapons detection systems in all general acute care and psychiatric hospitals in 2027.

catalysts.event: Resignation of executive officer Nasim Golzadeh
catalysts.sentence: On March 6, 2026, one of our executive officers, Nasim Golzadeh, notified us of her resignation as Managing Director, TechnoLogic and Executive Vice President, Investigative Solutions, effective March 31, 2026.

catalysts.event: Partnership to launch PlateRanger ALPR solution
catalysts.sentence: In July 2024, we announced a strategic partnership to create and launch a new end-to-end vehicle and ALPR public safety solution, PlateRanger, Powered by Rekor.

catalysts.event: Non-renewal of contract with City of Chicago
catalysts.sentence: The City of Chicago did not renew our contract in November 2024.
